Post-script, four years later. I sat in the audience for the college graduation ceremony. I thought my heart would burst with pride as my eldest daughter rose to her feet to give the valedictorian a dress. Today, I would like to pass along a gift that has been extremely valuable to me, she began. It's a gift that has made it possible for me to attend this wonderful college when I didn't think I would be able to. This gift is the secret behind my academic success at this fine institution, and subsequent nomination is your valedictorian. An honor I take seriously. And this same gift even helped my dad to find love after his heartbreak of losing my mom. Donna, sitting beside me, glanced over at me and squeezed my hand as we both remembered that day in the cafe. As you begin this exciting new chapter in your personal and professional lives, I know you will find this gift a very precious tool. One that could very well change your life for the better, just as it did the lives of me and my family. You've probably heard about how you should think outside the box, but now I'd like to show you how to think inside the box.
